questions.text: What is the least number which when divided by 15, 18, and 36 leaves the same remainder 9 in each case and is also completely divisible by 11?
If $x^2 - 3x + 1 = 0$, find the value of $x^5 + \frac{1}{x^5}$.
If $\cos\theta = \frac{2p}{1+p^2}$ and $0^\circ < \theta < 90^\circ$, find the value of $\tan\theta$.

In a circle with center O, an arc ADC subtends an angle of $132^\circ$ at the center of the circle. A chord AB is drawn and produced to a point P on the outside. If ABCD forms a cyclic quadrilateral, then what is the measure of $\angle CBP$?
By selling an article for Rs. 1170, a man suffers as much loss as he would have gained by selling it at a profit of 22%. What is the cost price of the article?
A can do $\frac{1}{4}$ of a work in 9 days, and B can do $\frac{2}{3}$ of the same work in 28 days. Working together, in how many days will they complete the whole work?

The average marks of 40 students in a class is 68. Later it was found that the marks of two students were incorrectly entered as 48 and 64 instead of 84 and 46 respectively. What is the correct average marks of the class?

The simple interest on a certain sum for $3\frac{1}{2}$ years at 10% p.a. is Rs. 2340 less than the compound interest on the same sum for $2\frac{1}{2}$ years at 20% p.a. compounded yearly. Find the sum.

A solid metallic cylinder of base radius $3\text{ cm}$ and height $4\text{ cm}$ is melted to form identical solid cones of height $1\text{ cm}$ and base radius $1\text{ mm}$. Find the number of cones so formed.
The lengths of the three sides of a triangle are $13\text{ cm}$, $14\text{ cm}$, and $15\text{ cm}$. Find the radius of its inscribed circle.
A train running at a speed of $72\text{ km/h}$ crosses a vertical pole in 12 seconds. How much time will it take to completely cross a bridge that is $360\text{ m}$ long?
The ratio of the present ages of A and B is 8:15. Eight years ago, the ratio of their ages was 6:13. What will be the ratio of their ages 8 years from now?
If $a^3 + b^3 + c^3 - 3abc = 250$ and $a + b + c = 10$, find the value of $ab + bc + ca$.
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point on the ground is $30^\circ$. If the point is $45\text{ m}$ away from the foot of the tower, what is the height of the tower?

In $\triangle ABC$, $D$ and $E$ are points on sides $AB$ and $AC$ respectively such that $DE \parallel BC$. If $AD = 2\text{ cm}$, $BD = 3\text{ cm}$, and the Area of $\triangle ADE$ is $12\text{ cm}^2$, find the total area of $\triangle ABC$.
The population of a town increases by 10% in the first year and then decreases by 20% in the second year. If the present population of the town is 44,000, what was the population two years ago?
A milkman mixes water with pure milk such that he gains a profit of 25% by selling the resulting mixture at the cost price of the pure milk. What is the ratio of water to milk in the mixture?
A boat can cover $24\text{ km}$ upstream in 4 hours and $36\text{ km}$ downstream in 3 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water.
What is the remainder when $7^{42}$ is divided by 48?
If $2x + y = 6$ and $xy = 4$, find the value of $8x^3 + y^3$.
ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral such that AB is the diameter of the circle circumscribing it and $\angle ADC = 142^\circ$. What is the measure of $\angle BAC$?
Find the value of the expression: $$\frac{\sin 30^\circ \cos 60^\circ + \cos 30^\circ \sin 60^\circ}{\tan 45^\circ}$$

Pipe A can fill an empty tank in 12 hours, Pipe B in 15 hours, and Pipe C can empty the completely filled tank in 20 hours. If all three pipes are opened simultaneously, in how much time will the empty tank be completely filled?

A dishonest shopkeeper marks his goods up by 20% and then gives a discount of 10%. Additionally, he uses a faulty balance which reads $1000\text{ g}$ for every $900\text{ g}$. What is his net profit percentage?
Simplify the following expression: $$5 \div 5 \text{ of } 5 \times 2 + 2 \div 2 \text{ of } 2 \times 5 - (5-2) \div 6 \times 2$$
